title: "The Vonage Dev Discussion: Making mistakes"
published: true

---

Nobody is perfect! Even the most seasoned developers have made mistakes – it’s all just part of the learning process.

One mistake that is so common in software development is accidentally pushing secrets to a public repository 🙈🙉🙊

{% embed https://vonage.dev/41PGNGN %}

Fortunately, there are lots of different guardrails you can put in place to help prevent the exposure of sensitive information like API keys, and we’ve covered a few of them in [these posts about environment variables](https://vonage.dev/48R3sWS).

But we’re curious: **What are some mistakes you’ve made as a developer? What did you learn from those mistakes?**

![A stylized illustration of the Vonage mascot with the text: What are some mistakes you’ve made as a developer? What did you learn from those mistakes?](https://dev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/articles/b9bjudjoqch6zqyh7vtd.png)

Share your wisdom in the comments below! 🙏👇
